This environmental toxin angle is particularly powerful because:

It's completely unexpected and attention-grabbing

Addresses something people use daily without thinking about it

Much better! Now the pattern interrupt stays focused on the cleaning product angle and makes logical sense. It addresses the common assumptions people have about "safer" cleaning alternatives:

Fragrance-free - people think removing fragrance makes products safe

"Natural" products - the quotation marks hint that marketing claims aren't always true

Eco-friendly laundry pods - targets the premium "better for you" products people switch to

This creates much stronger logical flow while maintaining the curiosity and pattern interrupt structure. The hook now builds properly toward revealing that even the products people think are "safe" alternatives are still problematic for blood sugar control.
